project_structure: Monorepo at /Users/wepie/Desktop/personal-projects/pcf with packages/{cli,server,shared}. The Cursor hook config wiring lives entirely inside packages/cli: (a) shipped JSON fragment template at packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/cursor-hooks.json (361 bytes, 4 entries in configs/), (b) installer/merger at packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts (mergeCursorHookConfig + HOOK_CONFIG_TARGETS.cursor + HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS.cursor + FABRIC_HOOK_COMMAND_PATHS.cursor), (c) symmetric uninstaller at packages/cli/src/install/uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts (unmergeCursorHookConfig + extractFlatCommands), (d) deep-merge engine at packages/cli/src/config/json.ts (arrayAppendPaths dotted-path array-append-with-dedupe), (e) post-install path validator at packages/cli/src/commands/hooks.ts (validateHookPaths iterates clients=[claude, codex, cursor]). Tests at packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts (8 integration tests asserting cursorHooks.events.{Stop,SessionStart,PreToolUse} shape) and packages/cli/__tests__/hooks-install-validate.test.ts (cross-client path validation). CLI version is currently 2.0.0-rc.13 per packages/cli/package.json — rc.14 is the next bump.

relevant_files.rationale: THE bug source. Ships {"events":{Stop,SessionStart,PreToolUse:[...]}} but Cursor's schema validator rejects this with 'Config version must be a number; Config hooks must be an object'. This template is deep-merged into the user's .cursor/hooks.json verbatim, so its top-level shape IS the user-facing schema. The 'events.X' envelope was copied from codex-hooks.json (see README.md L31: 'Mirrors the Codex events.Stop[] envelope shape') without verifying Cursor accepts it — a regression introduced in rc.5 TASK-010.

key_code.description: WRONG: top-level key is 'events' wrapping per-event arrays. Cursor expects top-level 'version' (number) and 'hooks' (object). Entries inside the arrays match the codex flat shape ({command} or {matcher, command}) which is also likely wrong for Cursor — its per-hook entry shape needs confirmation from official docs.

relevant_files.topic_relation: cursor-hooks-schema exploration: this is the single byte-on-disk source of the broken schema. Every fix path (template-rewrite, merge-logic-extension, or post-merge-injection) must rewrite this file's top-level shape.

relevant_files.rationale: Contains mergeCursorHookConfig (L476-L488) which is the function that deep-merges the broken template into .cursor/hooks.json, plus the three Cursor-specific source-of-truth constants HOOK_CONFIG_TARGETS.cursor (L123), HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS.cursor (L138 with 'events.Stop|SessionStart|PreToolUse'), and FABRIC_HOOK_COMMAND_PATHS.cursor (L158-L162). If the fix changes the schema from events.X to hooks.X, HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS.cursor MUST be updated in lockstep — the dotted paths are what enable array-append-with-dedupe in deepMerge.

key_code.description: Currently ['events.Stop', 'events.SessionStart', 'events.PreToolUse'] — dotted paths used by deepMerge to identify which arrays to append-with-dedupe instead of replace. MUST be changed in lockstep with template top-level key change (e.g. to 'hooks.Stop' etc) or the array-append behavior breaks and every re-install creates duplicates.

Reads cursor-hooks.json template + deep-merges into projectRoot/.cursor/hooks.json with arrayAppendPaths=HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS.cursor. Does NOT inject any top-level scalar like 'version' — current merge mechanism can only deep-merge whatever shape the template already has.

Generic merge engine: deepMerge(existing, fragment, {arrayAppendPaths}). Since deepMerge handles top-level primitives via REPLACE (json.ts L58-L67 — when either side is a primitive, returns source), a 'version: 1' top-level field in the template WILL be merged into the user's hooks.json correctly. No special-case logic needed for the version field IF it's included in the template.

relevant_files.topic_relation: cursor-hooks-schema exploration: this file holds the four constants whose values must stay byte-aligned with cursor-hooks.json. Any schema-shape fix is a multi-line edit here, not just a template swap.

relevant_files.rationale: Defines deepMerge (L35-L37) + arrayAppendPaths semantics + extractHookCommand (L103-L122). Critical to the fix because: (1) deepMerge replaces primitives via 'return source' at L66 — so a new top-level 'version: 1' in the template gets merged cleanly without any code change; (2) extractHookCommand reads {command} from top-level OR descends into {hooks:[{command}]} — so changing Cursor's per-entry shape to nested {hooks:[{...}]} would still dedupe correctly; (3) arrayAppendPaths takes dotted strings, so renaming events.Stop → hooks.Stop is purely a string-constant change.

key_code.description: Walks both trees in parallel; arrays at arrayAppendPaths get append-with-dedupe (L49-L56), all other arrays/primitives REPLACE with source (L58-L67). Critical: this is generic — does NOT know about hooks vs events vocabulary. The schema vocabulary is entirely owned by HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS constants in skills-and-hooks.ts.

Dedupe key extractor — returns entry.command if string, else descends into entry.hooks[].command. This works for BOTH Codex flat shape AND Claude Code nested {matcher,hooks:[{command}]} shape, so it would still work if Cursor schema turns out to need the Claude-style nested envelope.

relevant_files.topic_relation: cursor-hooks-schema exploration: deepMerge is shape-agnostic — it can merge any schema into the user's hooks.json correctly, including a top-level 'version' number. NO code changes needed in this file to fix the schema; the bug is purely in the template constants and HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS.

relevant_files.rationale: Symmetric inverse: contains unmergeCursorHookConfig (L222-L235) which uses the SAME HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS.cursor + FABRIC_HOOK_COMMAND_PATHS.cursor constants + extractFlatCommands (L657-L663) to prune fabric entries. If the install template moves from events.X to hooks.X, this uninstall path MUST update in lockstep (via the shared constants — no separate edit needed because it imports HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS). extractFlatCommands assumes top-level entry.command — if Cursor's entry shape turns out to be nested (Claude-style), this extractor needs swapping for extractClaudeCommands.

key_code.description: Reverse of mergeCursorHookConfig. Imports HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS.cursor + FABRIC_HOOK_COMMAND_PATHS.cursor from skills-and-hooks.ts — single source of truth. Uses extractFlatCommands as the entry-shape adapter.

Reads top-level entry.command. Works for current Codex/Cursor flat shape. If Cursor schema turns out to require the Claude nested shape, the uninstall side needs to switch to extractClaudeCommands (already defined at L631).

relevant_files.topic_relation: cursor-hooks-schema exploration: any schema fix on the install side creates a mirror obligation here. The shared-constants pattern minimizes the diff: changing HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS.cursor in skills-and-hooks.ts auto-propagates here, but the extractCommands choice (flat vs nested) is hardcoded at L232 and must be revisited.

relevant_files.rationale: Test 1 (L113-L221) explicitly asserts cursorHooks.events.{Stop,SessionStart,PreToolUse} shape after fresh init (L207-L220). Every assertion that reads cursorHooks.events.X must be updated to cursorHooks.hooks.X (or whatever final shape) when the schema is fixed. Also asserts the PreToolUse matcher contains 'Edit|Write|MultiEdit' (L220) — the per-entry shape stays compatible if Cursor accepts the codex flat {matcher, command} per-entry format.

key_code.description: Reads .cursor/hooks.json, parses, then asserts events.Stop|SessionStart|PreToolUse are arrays containing the expected hook-script paths. After the fix, these assertions become hooks.Stop|... AND a new version-field assertion (e.g. expect(cursorHooks.version).toBe(1)) is required to lock the schema.

Snapshots .claude and .codex trees only; does NOT snapshot .cursor. The fix should expand the snapshot to include .cursor to detect future regressions (or add an explicit byte-identical assertion on .cursor/hooks.json across re-runs).

relevant_files.topic_relation: cursor-hooks-schema exploration: this test file is the regression gate. Without updating its expectations, the failing fix would still pass CI under the old (broken) shape. The 'expect events.X' assertions ARE the bug, encoded in test form — fixing the template without fixing this test would just hide the issue from CI.

relevant_files.rationale: Tests installHooks() command's post-install path validation for all three clients including cursor (L58, L62, L66). Does NOT assert on cursor-hooks.json content schema — only on the existence of the hook .cjs script files. So this test is largely unaffected by the schema fix, BUT it does exercise the full install path, so if the merge fails outright due to schema mismatch the path-validation step would also surface 'missing-config' for cursor. Verify the test still passes after the fix.
relevant_files.topic_relation: cursor-hooks-schema exploration: secondary test surface — verifies the install pipeline doesn't crash with the corrected schema, but does not pin the schema shape itself.

relevant_files.rationale: Exercises the deepMerge engine including 'events.Stop' arrayAppendPaths (L67-68) and multi-path config (L114). No cursor-specific test BUT the engine is shared. After the fix, if the dotted path string for cursor changes from events.X to hooks.X, the existing deep-merge tests don't need updates (they test the generic engine), but a NEW test case might be added asserting the top-level 'version' number is preserved through merge — that's a deepMerge.test.ts addition, not edit.
relevant_files.topic_relation: cursor-hooks-schema exploration: validates the merge primitive that the cursor fix relies on; minor surface — likely an additive test case for top-level scalar preservation rather than edits.

relevant_files.rationale: Reference template — identical events.Stop|SessionStart|PreToolUse envelope to cursor-hooks.json. Confirms the events.X shape was copied from Codex into Cursor without verification (README.md L31). Codex's events.X shape is correct for Codex CLI; Cursor having the SAME shape is the bug. Compare-and-contrast file to anchor the fix scope: only cursor-hooks.json changes, codex-hooks.json stays.
relevant_files.topic_relation: cursor-hooks-schema exploration: anti-pattern reference — shows what NOT to copy into the cursor template anymore. Keeps the comparison concrete: codex stays at events.X, cursor moves to hooks.X (or similar).

relevant_files.rationale: Reference template for what 'hooks.X' looks like with matcher-wrapped envelopes: {hooks:{Stop:[{matcher:'*',hooks:[{type:'command',command:'...'}]}]}}. Claude Code uses 'hooks' as the top-level key (not 'events') and wraps each command in a matcher-envelope with nested hooks array. STRONG hypothesis: Cursor's schema is identical or very similar to Claude Code's, because the user-facing error 'Config hooks must be an object' implies Cursor wants top-level 'hooks: {...}'. If true, the fix is to make cursor-hooks.json structurally identical to claude-code.json with the cursor paths swapped in.

key_code.description: Top-level 'hooks' object → per-event array → matcher-wrapper object → inner 'hooks' array → {type:'command', command:'.claude/hooks/...'}. If Cursor follows this same convention, the fix is a near-mechanical port of this template structure with .claude/ → .cursor/ path substitution + adding a top-level 'version: 1' field.

relevant_files.topic_relation: cursor-hooks-schema exploration: the most likely target shape. The user error message ('hooks must be an object') is verbatim consistent with the Claude Code shape. Recommended fix shape: {version:1, hooks:{Stop:[{matcher:'*',hooks:[{type:'command',command:'.cursor/hooks/fabric-hint.cjs'}]}], ...}}.

relevant_files.rationale: Documents the design intent of each client template. L28-L34 ('cursor-hooks.json' section) explicitly says cursor 'Mirrors the Codex events.Stop[] envelope shape — Cursor's hook event vocabulary is not stable across releases'. This comment IS the root cause documentation — it admits Cursor schema was assumed-equal-to-Codex without verification. Must be rewritten in lockstep with the schema fix.

key_code.description: Documentation block that needs full rewrite to describe the new (correct) Cursor schema with version + hooks fields. Currently states the wrong design assumption verbatim.

relevant_files.topic_relation: cursor-hooks-schema exploration: documentation surface that drove the bug — the design intent comment justified copying Codex shape. Fixing the README closes the knowledge loop so future contributors don't reintroduce the regression.

relevant_files.rationale: Hosts the installHooks command + validateHookPaths post-step. validateHookPaths (around L160-L215) iterates clients=[claude, codex, cursor] and only checks for hook-script presence — does NOT validate JSON schema. So this file does NOT need changes for the schema fix UNLESS rc.14 wants to add a schema-validation step (out of scope for stop-the-bleeding).
relevant_files.topic_relation: cursor-hooks-schema exploration: validates the bug doesn't extend here. Touching this file is OUT of scope for the rc.14 stop-the-bleeding fix; mentioned only to confirm validation surface boundaries.

relevant_files.rationale: Currently at version 2.0.0-rc.13. The rc.14 fix requires bumping to 2.0.0-rc.14. Per project memory, v2.0.0 has never been published, so the rc-chain continues.
relevant_files.topic_relation: cursor-hooks-schema exploration: minor housekeeping — version bump must accompany the schema fix release.

relevant_files.rationale: Workspace's own installed .cursor/hooks.json — confirms what the broken template produces when merged into an empty config: exactly the same {events:{...}} shape as the template (no merge, fresh install). Useful as a fixture reference but is not a code file to modify (it's a regenerated artifact of running fab install on this very repo).
relevant_files.topic_relation: cursor-hooks-schema exploration: self-dogfood evidence of the bug. After the fix, running 'fab install' here regenerates this file in the new (correct) shape.

patterns: 1) Template-fragment + deep-merge pattern: each client has a JSON fragment template that ships in packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/; the install path deep-merges (with arrayAppendPaths) into the live config. 2) Dotted-path arrayAppend constants: HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS in skills-and-hooks.ts L135-L139 is the SINGLE source of truth for which JSON paths get array-append-with-dedupe — shared by both install (skills-and-hooks.ts) and uninstall (uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts). 3) Per-client command-string registry: FABRIC_HOOK_COMMAND_PATHS L147-L163 records the exact .cursor/.codex/.claude script paths used as dedupe keys + uninstall match keys. 4) Per-client extractor strategy: extractFlatCommands (codex/cursor) vs extractClaudeCommands (claude-code, descends one level into nested hooks[]). 5) Idempotent install via byte-comparison: copyTextIdempotent + mergeJsonIdempotent compare existing to target before writing — re-runs produce zero diffs. 6) Three-event symmetry: every install/uninstall code path iterates [Stop, SessionStart, PreToolUse] (rc.5 added Stop, rc.6 added the two siblings). 7) Test-asserts-schema pattern: integration tests parse the produced JSON and assert top-level shape (events.Stop array, matcher contains 'Edit|Write|MultiEdit'), making schema changes test-visible.
dependencies: External: @fenglimg/fabric-shared/node/atomic-write (atomicWriteJson with indent:2). Internal: packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts → packages/cli/src/config/json.ts (deepMerge). packages/cli/src/install/uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts → packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts (imports HOOK_CONFIG_TARGETS, HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS, FABRIC_HOOK_COMMAND_PATHS, HOOK_SCRIPT_DESTINATIONS, SKILL_DESTINATIONS, FABRIC_SECTION_REGEX, SECTION_TARGETS — single source of truth). packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts → mergeCursorHookConfig (bootstrap stage). packages/cli/src/commands/hooks.ts → mergeCursorHookConfig + validateHookPaths (re-install command). Template file → loaded via readJsonTemplate at runtime (no compile-time link). No external schema validator (Zod/JSON Schema) is wired into the Cursor hooks flow — schema correctness is enforced only by Cursor itself at runtime.
integration_points: 1) packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/cursor-hooks.json (FILE) — schema source; rewrite top-level from {events:{...}} to {version:<number>, hooks:{...}}. 2) packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts:138 HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS.cursor — change ['events.Stop', 'events.SessionStart', 'events.PreToolUse'] to ['hooks.Stop', 'hooks.SessionStart', 'hooks.PreToolUse'] in lockstep with template. 3) packages/cli/src/install/uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts:232 extractCommands — possibly switch from extractFlatCommands to extractClaudeCommands if the entry shape moves to nested {matcher,hooks:[{command}]}. 4) packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts:207-221 — rewrite cursorHooks.events.X assertions to cursorHooks.hooks.X + add cursorHooks.version assertion. 5) packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/README.md:28-34 — rewrite cursor section to document the new correct schema. 6) packages/cli/package.json:3 — bump version 2.0.0-rc.13 → 2.0.0-rc.14. The deepMerge engine in packages/cli/src/config/json.ts does NOT need changes: a top-level scalar like 'version: 1' in the template is merged via primitive-replace (json.ts L58-L67 'return source') which correctly preserves it when no prior value exists in user file, and overwrites stale values on re-install.
constraints: 1) Cursor's exact hooks.json schema is not in this repo and the readme admits it was guessed — primary uncertainty is whether the entry shape is flat {command} (Codex style) or nested {matcher, hooks:[{type:'command', command}]} (Claude Code style). The user error message 'Config hooks must be an object' only locks down that top-level 'hooks' must be an object — it does NOT confirm per-entry shape. 2) Merge mechanism limitation: deepMerge handles top-level scalar (version: 1) via REPLACE — this means if a user already has a different version number, re-running fab install OVERWRITES it. This may be desirable for version coupling but should be documented. 3) arrayAppendPaths requires a STRING match on the dotted path — template top-level key and HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS.cursor MUST agree byte-for-byte or the array-append falls back to array-REPLACE silently (json.ts L49-L56 — falls through to L58-L67 if path mismatch), which would WIPE user-authored hook entries on re-install. 4) Dedupe key extraction (extractHookCommand in json.ts L103-L122) handles BOTH flat {.command} and nested {.hooks:[{.command}]} — so the install dedupe works regardless of which Cursor entry shape turns out to be correct. The uninstall side (extractFlatCommands vs extractClaudeCommands) is the hardcoded asymmetry that needs deliberate selection. 5) Cursor's hooks docs are not stable across releases (per README.md L33), so any schema fix has a finite shelf life — a follow-up rc may need to revisit.

clarification_needs.question: What is Cursor's authoritative hooks.json schema? Specifically: is the per-event entry shape flat ({command} or {matcher, command} — Codex style) or nested ({matcher, hooks:[{type:'command', command}]} — Claude Code style)?
clarification_needs.context: The user error message 'Config version must be a number; Config hooks must be an object' confirms two top-level requirements: (a) 'version' field exists and is a number, (b) top-level 'hooks' key is an object. But the per-entry shape inside hooks.Stop|SessionStart|PreToolUse arrays is NOT pinned by the error message. Picking the wrong shape will still trigger a Cursor validator error after the fix lands. The repo has no link to Cursor official docs and the README.md cursor section explicitly admits the original shape was assumed not verified.
options: Claude Code style: hooks.<event> = [{matcher, hooks: [{type:'command', command}]}] — exact mirror of templates/hooks/configs/claude-code.json with .claude/ paths swapped to .cursor/
Codex flat style: hooks.<event> = [{command}] or [{matcher, command}] — keep current per-entry shape, only rename top-level events → hooks and add version field
Hybrid / unknown — fetch Cursor's official hooks.json docs via Exa MCP or web fetch before committing to a shape
Conservative: implement Claude Code style + add a runtime schema validator that surfaces a clear error if Cursor rejects (defense-in-depth)

clarification_needs.question: Should the 'version' field in cursor-hooks.json template be hardcoded as version:1, or should it be data-driven (e.g. read from a Cursor-version-detection step at install time)?
clarification_needs.context: The user error explicitly says 'Config version must be a number' — so a literal number is required. Cursor may version its hooks schema over time. Today the only known major version is 1 (current). Future Cursor releases may bump to 2 with breaking changes. Hardcoding 1 is correct for today but creates a maintenance burden. Detection-based version selection is over-engineering for a stop-the-bleeding fix.
options: Hardcode version: 1 in cursor-hooks.json template (simplest, ships today)
Hardcode version: 1 + add a TODO comment + a CHANGELOG note tracking Cursor schema versions
Detection-based: at install time read Cursor's app version and pick template version accordingly (premature)

clarification_needs.question: When merging the new template into a user's existing .cursor/hooks.json, what should happen if the user has a DIFFERENT 'version' number (e.g. user has version:2, template ships version:1)?
clarification_needs.context: deepMerge's default REPLACE semantics for primitives means the template's version field OVERWRITES the user's. This is the most likely correct behavior because fabric ships hook scripts that target a specific schema version. But if Cursor's schema is backward-compat and the user upgraded their hooks.json to v2 manually, an overwrite could regress them.
options: Always overwrite with template's version (current default deepMerge behavior — no code changes)
Preserve user's version if higher than template's (requires custom merge logic — out of scope for stop-the-bleeding)
Warn and skip if version mismatch detected (requires schema-aware merge — large new surface)

_metadata.task_description: rc.14 Stop the bleeding — fix Cursor hooks.json schema (current template writes {events:{...}}, Cursor expects {version:<number>, hooks:{...}}). User error: 'Config version must be a number; Config hooks must be an object' in werewolf-minigame project.
